data_json.content:
Yes, if you mean a target-scoped sync operation. The CLI already supports one-target rendering and checking through generate-target, but sync-targets itself is config-wide: its args only carry config, dry_run, and check, and the implementation walks every target in the loaded config, computes stale outputs for the whole config, and deletes stale generated records at config scope. That means there is no existing selector for “sync just this one target” in sync-targets.

If you mean updating the canonical rules from the current contents of a generated file, that is a different reverse flow. The nearest existing command is import-file, but it imports markdown blocks into rule storage and is not target-aware, so it does not behave like a single-target sync from an existing generated artifact.

For your exact roast target, the mapping is already explicit in [rule-targets/45-agents-agents.yaml](rule-targets/45-agents-agents.yaml#L58) and the generated output lives at [./.agents/agents/roast.agent.md](./.agents/agents/roast.agent.md#L1). If you want the existing single-target generation path, the relevant entrypoint is [generate_target_command](memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/dispatch_secondary.rs#L43), and the selector behavior is covered by [generate_target_supports_directory_config](memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/tests/tests_generate_targets.rs#L4) and [generate_target_uses_config_output_path](memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/tests/tests_commands.rs#L499).

What would define a proper single-target sync feature is mostly:
- selector syntax: target name, output path, or both
- stale-output behavior: remove only the selected target’s stale record, or suppress stale cleanup entirely
- bookkeeping: whether generated-target records should be updated per target or per config
- spec-doc handling: whether .spec outputs keep using the spec-store path and cache behavior
- reverse-direction semantics: whether the command should sync from rules to file, or import from file to rules

Relevant anchors:
- [SyncTargetsArgs](memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/args.rs#L217)
- [sync_targets_command](memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/dispatch_secondary.rs#L93)
- [sync_targets_payload](memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/rendering.rs#L294)
- stale-output removal logic in [rendering.rs](memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/rendering.rs#L420)
- command overview in [rule-cli README](memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/README.md#L17)
- reverse import flow in [import_file](memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/importing.rs#L24)
- import-file test coverage in [import_file_creates_rules_from_markdown_blocks](memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/tests/tests_commands.rs#L442)
- workspace/target topology context in [e815f261 spec](memory-api/.spec/specs/e815f261-ca7d-4957-8f68-666e1e1dfbfe/body.md#L5) and [050c5441 ticket](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/050c5441-1d3a-46bc-9748-cfb7030a93bd/description.md#L1)

If you want, I can turn this into a concrete behavior spec for “single-target rule sync” next.
